title: Vilmarh Grahrk
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Grahrk}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Vilmarh.jpg]] |name=Vilmarh Grahrk |homeworld=[[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]][[Star Wars (1998) 19|''Star Wars'' (1998) 19]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him''[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 4]]'' |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Red]] |skin=Red |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Vilmarh Grahrk is the luckiest Devaronian who ever lived.''"
"''You forget—also handsomest.|[[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] and Vilmarh Grahrk|[[Republic 82|''Republic'' 82]]}} '''Vilmarh Grahrk''', or '''Villie''' as he was often known, was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the early days of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. A notorious [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]], [[Con artist/Legends|grifter]] and [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrel]], Villie took part in a number of underhanded schemes in his quest for money and women. ==Biography== One of Grahrk's first schemes involved working for no less than [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] himself. During his recruitment, he insulted [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], earning a brief [[Force power/Legends#Choking powers|Force choke]] from the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. Together with his cousin [[Holmar Grahrk|Holmar]], Villie was tasked with inciting the [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] into battle with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. The [[Yinchorri Uprising|resulting conflict]] left a number of Jedi—including [[Jedi Council/Legends|Council]] member [[Micah Giiett]]—dead, and Holmar captured. Grahrk was later questioned by the Jedi, but was deemed to know nothing of value. [[File:VilmarhGrahrk.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Villie]] Grahrk next came to the Jedi's attention when he encountered the amnesiac [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. At this time Grahrk had once again gambled away his money, which he made from transporting the [[glitteryll]] that had wiped the minds of Vos and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]. Grahrk saw Vos as an opportunity to break even, and placed the bet with the longest odds of all—that Vos would survive long enough to get away from the Smugglers' Moon. Then, in a series of double and triple-crosses, Grahrk escaped with Vos on his ship, the [[Inferno (Vilmarh Grahrk)|''Inferno'']]. From Nar Shaddaa, Grahrk took Vos to [[Kiffu/Legends|Kiffu]], [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] and [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. During this time, Grahrk killed Holmar and double-crossed the corrupt politician [[Chom Frey Kaa]] who had begun the glitteryll scandal. Grahrk was next hired to smuggle weapons to the convicts on [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]], but was shot down by the [[Kiffu Guardians]] and imprisoned himself. He was found by no less than Quinlan Vos and his [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] in their hunt for the rogue Aayla Secura. Grahrk was uncomfortable being in the presence of two Jedi, and this discomfort only grew with the addition of Masters [[Zao]] and [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]]. Although the Jedi would not allow Grahrk to leave the planet before the defeat of [[Volfe Karkko]], Quinlan Vos did agree to travel with him as he followed the will of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Grahrk's also had a part in [[Kh'aris Fenn]]'s plot to take control of Ryloth. Grahrk was hired as a pilot for the [[Morgukai]] [[Tsyr]] and [[Bok]] when they kidnapped [[Nat Secura]] and Tholme. Vos and Secura tracked Villie to [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]], hoping that he would reveal where he had taken the prisoners. With typical faithfulness to his employers, Grahrk told the Jedi everything he knew—though not before they had used the Force to help him win some dice rolls. Of course, providing aid to the Jedi didn't stop him for also working for his original employers. Grahrk spirited Fenn away from Ryloth before he was made to walk in the Bright Lands, saving both of them from serious trouble. [[File:Villie_With_Vos.jpg|thumb|200px|Villie with [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]]] Sometime during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Grahrk angered [[Durge/Legends|Durge]]. Perhaps due to this, in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], Grahrk came to the [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] village of [[Palsaang/Legends|Palsaang]], and was allowed to stay as he smuggled goods onto the world. During this time he befriended [[Chak (Wookiee)|Chak]], who began calling him "Uncle Villie". When the [[Battle of Kashyyyk/Legends|Battle of Kashyyyk]] came, Grahrk saw Quinlan Vos and others fighting for their lives against [[LM-432 crab droid/Legends|LM-432 crab droids]] and [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]]. Grahrk and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] friend Chak swooped down in the ''Inferno'' and saved Jedi Vos, [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]], and other [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] and Wookiees. After that, Vos attempted to contact [[Khaleen Hentz/Legends|Khaleen Hentz]] but the tall trees caused interference with the [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]]. Grahrk let Vos use his on the ''Inferno''. But Vos discovered Grahrk had been smuggling Wookiee hidden hyperspace routes. He was about to take Grahrk in when it turned out Chak showed him the routes so Grahrk didn't steal them. When the actual battle of Kashyyyk came, Grahrk decided to fight for the Wookiees but was shot down by a [[HMP droid gunship/Legends|Heavy Missile Platform]]. Grahrk and Chak were right in the path of a [[NR-N99 Persuader-class droid enforcer/Legends|Corporate Alliance Tank Droid]], but were saved by Vos' [[HAVw A6 Juggernaut/Legends|Juggernaut]]. Grahrk would soon set out to return the favor, as [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] set Quinlan against [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Faie/Legends|Faie]] and Kashyyyk's entire contingent of clones. Grahrk found Vos broken and beaten along side the corpse of Commander Faie. Grahrk then burned the trooper's body, and when questioned by a Clone Patrol, said he killed and burned the Jedi, out of respect. He then proceeded to smuggle Vos off planet and aided the Jedi in his recovery. Eight months after the end of the war, Grahrk returned to Kashyyyk and was present at the reunion of Quinlan Vos and Khaleen.[[Republic 83|''Republic'' 83]] ==Behind the scenes== [[File:UncleVillie.jpg|thumb|"Uncle Villie" from ''[[Star Wars Infinities: The Empire Strikes Back]]'']] Vilmarh Grahrk was voiced by [[Tom Kane]] in the game ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' and appears in the game as a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] hero. His species functions as the Gungans' bounty hunter unit. {{Noncanonstart|legends=1}} At around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Han Solo and Chewbacca arrived in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] to meet Grahrk so that he could deliver their debt to Jabba. Unfortunately for them, Grahrk had other plans and captured Solo and the Wookiee and handed them over to Jabba before stealing the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. However, Solo and Chewbacca would escape and recapture the ''Falcon'' from Grahrk. {{Noncanonend}} ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Vilmarh Grahrk}} *''[[Star Wars:
